pois.name: Buarbreen carpark
pois.description: The starting point for the hike to the Buarbreen glacier, offering parking and direct access to the trail along the Jordalselvi river.

pois.name: Tjørnadalsfossen
pois.description: A magnificent waterfall located near Odda, featuring a series of drops that are particularly powerful during the spring melt.

pois.name: Buarbreen
pois.description: An outlet glacier of the massive Folgefonna glacier, famous for its accessible blue ice and dramatic valley views.

pois.name: Trolltunga Hike Starting Point
pois.description: The Skjeggedal trailhead, which serves as the primary gateway for the world-famous 28km round-trip hike to the 'Troll's Tongue'.

pois.name: Kjerag
pois.description: A mountain plateau in Lysefjord featuring the famous Kjeragbolten, a boulder wedged in a mountain crevasse 1,000 meters above the sea.

pois.name: Eidfjord
pois.description: A picturesque village at the head of the Eidfjorden, serving as a hub for visiting the Hardangervidda plateau and Vøringfossen.

pois.name: IKEA Forus
pois.description: A large commercial landmark located in the Forus business district between Stavanger and Sandnes.

pois.name: Rondane-Nationalpark
pois.description: Norway's oldest national park, characterized by high peaks, vast plateaus, and one of the country's last wild reindeer herds.

pois.name: Svandalsfoss
pois.description: An impressive waterfall near Sauda where the spray often reaches the road; it features a scenic staircase for hikers.

pois.name: Trollpikken
pois.description: A uniquely shaped rock formation near Egersund that has become a popular local hiking destination.

pois.name: Geirangerfjord
pois.description: A UNESCO World Heritage site known for its deep blue water, steep mountain walls, and famous waterfalls like the Seven Sisters.

pois.name: Vøringfossen
pois.description: One of Norway's most famous waterfalls, with a total drop of 182 meters from the Hardangervidda plateau into the Måbødalen valley.

pois.name: Nationalpark Fulufjället
pois.description: A Swedish national park home to Njupeskär, Sweden's highest waterfall, and 'Old Tjikko', one of the world's oldest trees.

pois.name: Fargegaten
pois.description: Stavanger's most colorful street (Øvre Holmegate), famous for its vibrant houses, quirky cafes, and boutique shops.

pois.name: Odda
pois.description: An industrial town turned tourism hub, nestled between the Hardangerfjord and the mountains of Folgefonna National Park.

pois.name: Langfoss
pois.description: Voted one of the world's most beautiful waterfalls, it cascades 612 meters down a cliffside directly into the Åkrafjord.

pois.name: Låtefossen
pois.description: A famous 'twin' waterfall where two separate streams join under a historic stone bridge on the road to Odda.

pois.name: Preikestolen
pois.description: Also known as Pulpit Rock, this flat-topped cliff towers 604 meters above the Lysefjord and is Norway's most iconic hike.

pois.name: Kanelstranda
pois.description: A beautiful sandy beach in Mandal, popular for swimming and coastal walks in Southern Norway.

pois.name: Brattfallet
pois.description: A high waterfall and canyon in Värmland, Sweden, featuring historical timber-floating trails.

pois.name: Städjan
pois.description: A distinctive volcano-shaped mountain in Dalarna, Sweden, offering 360-degree views of the mountain range.

pois.name: Helagsfjäll
pois.description: Sweden's highest peak south of the Arctic Circle, featuring a 24km legendary hiking circuit and a glacier.

pois.name: Tyngsjö
pois.description: A serene wilderness area in Dalarna, Sweden, perfect for off-grid camping and lake activities.

pois.name: Kloten
pois.description: A premier canoeing destination within the Malingsbo-Kloten Nature Reserve, Sweden.

pois.name: Konst på Hög
pois.description: A unique outdoor sculpture park located on top of a 100-meter high ash pile in Kumla, Sweden.

pois.name: Nora
pois.description: One of Sweden's best-preserved wooden towns, famous for its historic steam engine railway and local ice cream.

pois.name: Naturens hus
pois.description: A visitor center in Örebro located by Lake Hjälmaren, serving as a starting point for kayaking and nature walks.

pois.name: Wadköping
pois.description: An open-air museum in Örebro with buildings dating back to the 16th century, showcasing Swedish history.

pois.name: Fettjeåfallet
pois.description: One of Sweden's highest waterfalls (60m), reached by a beautiful stone-stepped path near Klövsjö.

pois.name: Haverdals Nature reserve
pois.description: A coastal reserve in Halland featuring one of Scandinavia's highest sand dunes, the Lynga dune.

pois.name: Tönnersa Naturreservat
pois.description: A vast coastal reserve with wide sandy beaches and massive dune systems south of Halmstad.

pois.name: Jakriborg
pois.description: A modern housing estate in Hjärup built in a surreal medieval Hanseatic style.

pois.name: Yngereds Kraftverk
pois.description: A historic hydroelectric power plant located along the Ätran river in Sweden.

pois.name: Gekås Ullared
pois.description: Scandinavia's largest department store and a major cultural phenomenon in Sweden.

pois.name: Sumpafallen
pois.description: A nature reserve featuring powerful rapids and beautiful oak forests along the Högvadsån river.

pois.name: Schloss Läckö
pois.description: A stunning baroque castle situated on a headland in Lake Vänern, Sweden.

pois.name: Färnebofjärdens nationalpark
pois.description: A unique river landscape where the Dalälven river meets the northern forest, rich in birdlife.

pois.name: Autofriedhof Kyrkö Mosse
pois.description: A famous 'car cemetery' in the woods of Småland where vintage cars are being reclaimed by nature.

pois.name: Blå Jungfrun nationalpark
pois.description: An isolated island in the Baltic Sea known for its granite domes, caves, and legendary folklore.

pois.name: Sarek
pois.description: Europe's last true wilderness; a high-alpine national park in Sweden with no marked trails or cabins.

pois.name: Ales stenar
pois.description: A megalithic monument in Skåne, Sweden, consisting of 59 large stones arranged in the shape of a ship.

pois.name: Åsnens Nationalpark
pois.description: A unique lake archipelago landscape in Sweden with old-growth forests and rich biodiversity.

pois.name: Döda fallet
pois.description: The 'Dead Fall' in Sweden, a dry waterfall created when a lake was accidentally drained in 1796.

pois.name: Storforsen
pois.description: One of the highest-volume rapids in Europe, located on the Pite River in Swedish Lapland.

pois.name: Nationalpark Tiveden
pois.description: A wild, rocky forest area in Sweden known for its giant boulders and crystal-clear lakes.

pois.name: Nationalpark Skuleskogen
pois.description: A High Coast park in Sweden where the mountains meet the sea, featuring the Slåttdalsskrevan canyon.

pois.name: Korallgrottan
pois.description: Sweden's longest cave system, a marble cave located in the wilderness of Jämtland.

pois.name: Skierfe
pois.description: A mountain in Sarek National Park offering the most famous view in Sweden, looking down over the Rapa River delta.
